title: Absolute Value as Distance from Zero
description: Interpret the absolute value of a number as its distance from 0 on the number line, regardless of sign.
condition: Expression of the form |a| where a is a real number.
response: State or compute the nonnegative distance of a from 0.
label: Principle / Model
example: |-150| = 150 because −150 is 150 units from 0.

title: Area of Rectangle from Coordinates
description: Calculate the area of a rectangle by multiplying side lengths found from coordinate differences.
condition: Vertices of a rectangle are given on a coordinate plane.
response: Compute horizontal length and vertical length, then multiply $l \times w$.
label: Principle / Model
example: Width $=8$, height $=10$ so area $=8 \times 10 = 80$ square units.

title: Classify Point by Quadrant Using Signs
description: Determine the quadrant of a point based on the signs of its x- and y-coordinates.
condition: Given an ordered pair (x, y) with x ≠ 0 and y ≠ 0.
response: Identify the quadrant: I if (+,+), II if (-,+), III if (-,-), IV if (+,-).
label: Concept
example: For $(-5.25, -6)$, both coordinates are negative, so the point is in Quadrant III.

title: Compare Absolute Values
description: Compare two numbers by first evaluating their absolute values and then using inequality symbols.
condition: Two expressions involving absolute value, such as |a| and |b|.
response: Evaluate each absolute value and insert <, >, or = to correctly compare them.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Integer to Zero
description: Determine whether a given integer is greater than, less than, or equal to 0.
condition: Given an integer (positive, negative, or zero) and asked whether it is above/below/equal to 0.
response: State whether the integer is > 0, < 0, or = 0.
label: Principle / Model
example: Determine if $10 > 0$. Since 10 is positive, $10 > 0$.

title: Compare Integers on Number Line
description: Determine which of two integers is greater by their positions on the number line.
condition: Two real numbers a and b to be compared.
response: Write a correct inequality (e.g., a < b) based on which is farther right on the number line.
label: Principle / Model
example: On the number line, −15 is left of −3, so −15 < −3.

title: Compare Rational Numbers
description: Compare integers, fractions, and decimals (including negatives) to determine their order.
condition: Two rational numbers (integers, fractions, or decimals) are given.
response: Determine and state which number is greater or less using >, <, or =.
label: Principle / Model
example: Because $-0.5$ and $-\frac{1}{2}$ represent the same value, $-0.5 = -\frac{1}{2}$.

title: Compute Absolute Value
description: Find the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line.
condition: A rational number is enclosed in absolute value symbols.
response: State its nonnegative distance from 0.
label: Concept

title: Compute Block (Manhattan) Distance
description: Find the distance between two points along grid lines by summing the absolute horizontal and vertical differences.
condition: Two points on a coordinate grid where movement is restricted to horizontal and vertical streets (blocks).
response: Calculate $|x_2 - x_1| + |y_2 - y_1|$.
label: Principle / Model
example: Distance between $(-4, 2)$ and $(3, 2)$ is $|3 - (-4)| + |2 - 2| = 7 + 0 = 7$ blocks.
notes: Used when grid represents city blocks.

title: Count Horizontal and Vertical Units on Coordinate Grid
description: Determine a point’s coordinates by counting units horizontally and vertically from the origin.
condition: Given a plotted point on a coordinate grid.
response: Count horizontal movement for x-coordinate and vertical movement for y-coordinate to state the ordered pair.
label: Production / Skill
example: If a point is 11 units right and 6 units up from the origin, its coordinates are $(11, 6)$.

title: Distance as Absolute Difference
description: Compute the distance between two elevations on a number line using absolute value of their difference.
condition: Two signed numbers representing positions or elevations are given and distance is requested.
response: Calculate $|a-b|$ to find the positive distance between them.
label: Principle / Model
example: The distance from $-15$ ft to $0$ ft is $|-15-0| = 15$ ft.

title: Find Horizontal or Vertical Distance
description: Determine the length of a horizontal or vertical segment by taking the absolute difference of the differing coordinate.
condition: Two points share the same x-coordinate (vertical segment) or same y-coordinate (horizontal segment).
response: Compute the absolute difference of the other coordinate.
label: Principle / Model
example: Distance between $(-7,8)$ and $(7,8)$ is $|7 - (-7)| = 14$.

title: Find Missing Vertices of Axis-Aligned Rectangle
description: Determine the remaining vertices of a rectangle with sides parallel to the axes using given vertices and side lengths.
condition: Two known vertices of an axis-aligned rectangle and given width/length.
response: Use horizontal and vertical distances to determine the other two coordinate pairs.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one vertex is $(3,6)$ and width is 8 vertically, another vertex could be $(3,-2)$ since $6-8=-2$.
notes: Assumes sides are parallel to axes (as shown in grid)???

title: Find the Opposite of a Number
description: Determine the opposite (additive inverse) of a number.
condition: A real number a.
response: State -a as its opposite.
label: Principle / Model
example: The opposite of $6$ is $-6$ because $6 + (-6) = 0$.

title: Identify Point at Given Block Distance
description: Select or generate a point that is a specified Manhattan distance from a given point.
condition: A reference point and a required block (Manhattan) distance are given.
response: Find a point $(x,y)$ such that $|x - x_0| + |y - y_0|$ equals the given distance.
label: Principle / Model
example: If school is at $(-2,9)$, a point 5 blocks away is $(1,9)$ since $|1 - (-2)| + |9 - 9| = 3 + 0 = 3$ ???
notes: Example distance arithmetic may vary depending on exact school coordinate ???

title: Identify Point on an Axis
description: Determine whether a point lies on the x-axis or y-axis based on a zero coordinate.
condition: Given an ordered pair (x, y).
response: If y = 0, the point is on the x-axis; if x = 0, the point is on the y-axis.
label: Concept
example: For $(0, -5)$, since $x = 0$, the point lies on the y-axis.

title: Interpret Directional Movement from Origin
description: Translate verbal directions (left/right/up/down and units) into coordinate changes.
condition: Given instructions to move a certain number of units in a cardinal direction from the origin.
response: Adjust x for left/right and y for up/down by the given number of units.
label: Principle / Model
example: From the origin, move 3 units right and 2 units up to reach $(3, 2)$.
notes: Right/left affect x; up/down affect y.

title: Interpret Signed Numbers in Context
description: Explain the meaning of positive, negative, or zero values in real-world contexts such as elevation, temperature, or bank balance.
condition: A signed number given within a contextual situation.
response: Describe what the sign and magnitude represent in context (e.g., below sea level, debt, colder temperature).
label: Principle / Model
example: An elevation of −15 feet means 15 feet below sea level.

title: Measure Distance from Point to Axis
description: Find the distance from a point to the x-axis or y-axis using absolute value of the relevant coordinate.
condition: Given a point (x, y) and asked for distance to an axis.
response: Distance to x-axis is |y|; distance to y-axis is |x|.
label: Principle / Model
example: For $(-5, -3)$, distance to the x-axis is $|-3| = 3$ and to the y-axis is $|-5| = 5$.

title: Negative Numbers Are Below Zero
description: Recognize that any negative number represents a value below zero in context.
condition: Given a real-world context with 0 as a reference point and a negative number.
response: Interpret the negative number as below the reference point.
label: Concept
example: Since $-15 < 0$, an elevation of $-15$ feet is below sea level.

title: Opposites Are Equidistant from Zero
description: Recognize that two numbers are opposites if they are the same distance from 0 on opposite sides.
condition: Given two numbers or labeled points on a number line and asked if they are opposites.
response: Determine whether one equals the negative of the other.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $d = 0.5$ and $c = -0.5$, then $c$ is the opposite of $d$ because $c = -d$.

title: Order Rational Numbers
description: Arrange integers, fractions, and decimals from least to greatest by comparing their values.
condition: A list of rational numbers in different forms.
response: Rewrite them in ascending order based on their numerical values.
label: Principle / Model
example: Since −4 < −2.9 < −12/8 < −3/5 < 1/4 < 9/10, this is the correct order from least to greatest.

title: Perimeter of Rectangle from Coordinates
description: Determine the perimeter of a rectangle by finding side lengths from coordinate differences and summing all sides.
condition: Four vertices of a rectangle are given on a coordinate plane.
response: Find horizontal and vertical side lengths using absolute differences and compute $2(l + w)$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Positive Numbers Are Above Zero
description: Recognize that any positive number represents a value above zero in context.
condition: Given a real-world context where 0 is a reference point (e.g., freezing, sea level) and a positive number.
response: Interpret the positive number as above the reference point.
label: Concept
example: Since $80 > 0$, $80^\circ\text{C}$ is above freezing (0°C).

title: Read Ordered Pair from Graph
description: Identify the coordinates of a plotted point by reading its horizontal (x) and vertical (y) position on the grid.
condition: A point is plotted on a coordinate plane.
response: State its ordered pair $(x, y)$.
label: Production / Skill
example: Point $F$ is located at $(-7, 8)$.
notes: Requires interpreting grid scale and axis orientation.

title: Read Value from Number Line Point
description: Determine the numerical value represented by a labeled point on a number line.
condition: Given a number line with evenly spaced tick marks and a labeled point.
response: State the numerical value of the point.
label: Production / Skill
example: If point $B$ is halfway between $1$ and $2$, then $B = 1.5$.

title: Reflect Point Across the x-Axis
description: Determine or recognize the image of a point after reflection across the x-axis.
condition: Given a point (x, y) and reflection across the x-axis.
response: Keep x the same and change y to its opposite: (x, -y).
label: Principle / Model
example: Reflecting $(6, 8)$ across the x-axis gives $(6, -8)$.
notes: Based on symmetry over the horizontal axis.

title: Simplify Double Negative
description: Simplify expressions involving a negative sign applied to a negative number.
condition: Given an expression of the form $-(-a)$.
response: Rewrite as $a$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Zero as Reference Point
description: Identify 0 as the reference value in contextual situations such as sea level or freezing point.
condition: Given a context (e.g., sea level, freezing point) and asked for the value representing the reference level.
response: State 0.
label: Concept
example: Sea level is represented by $0$ feet.

title: Zero Is Its Own Opposite
description: Recognize that the opposite of 0 is 0.
condition: Given the number 0 and asked for its opposite.
response: State 0.
label: Fact
example: The opposite of $0$ is $0$ because $-0 = 0$.
